comments.text_display:
So much that is not being said here for obvious reasons. Basic propaganda. Think critically. 

Notice how they don’t go deep on alignment/safety or mass unemployment. 
- AI security is essentially a fallacy due the near infinite attack surface regarding prompt injection
- job creation is happening in the short term. True. Long term no one has jobs. Humanoid electricians are obviously coming. Agentic radiologists, OBVIOUSLY coming. 

Once again, think critically. Dig into these issues and you’ll see what’s going on here. 

That said, he’s correct about AI replacing government but he talks about it like it’s not exactly going to happen. 

Also his timescales are ultra off. God-ish level ASI in decades. Ha! And why didn’t he mention anything about agentic AI research? 
That’s what’s likely going to cause FOOM and shortly after god level ASI. 

I think you’d be surprised what the private internal discussions look like. I think it’s one of those situations where you don’t want to cause panic in the population so you just sugarcoat everything. That’s likely what’s happening here as well as protecting their rep and business/investments.

comments.text_display:
"Physical AI" should properly be interpreted as "Embodied AI"—referring to artificial intelligence that possesses human common sense and a comprehension of the physical reality humans inhabit.
In essence, the definition of Physical AI necessitates that AI truly possess Human-Level intelligence and common sense, rather than merely performing binary mathematical reasoning based on zeros and ones.
It is perplexing why Taiwanese media consistently mistranslates this term as "Physics AI."
Embodied AI is not a challenge of mathematical deduction, but rather a "physical" challenge of endowing AI with human common sense. Even if NVIDIA’s GPUs were to increase in speed by a million-fold, would raw computational speed truly suffice to grant AI common sense?
Jensen Huang’s NVIDIA appears to be caught in the "Innovator's Dilemma": how can they penetrate the AI PC market without cannibalizing their established data center dominance?
It seems Mr. Huang has yet to formulate a strategy to resolve this dilemma. Instead, he appears to be leveraging the lofty concept of "Physical AI" to mislead the market, projecting an image that NVIDIA remains at the cutting edge of AI leadership.
Has Jensen Huang inadvertently risked positioning NVIDIA as little more than a fraudulent enterprise?
